content_en:
The delegation returned speechless to their leaders and told them what Abu Bakr Siddiq 4 said. On hearing this reply the chieftains were enraged and thought the followers of Islam present in AlMadinah would not be able to oppose them. Thus, this would be an opportune time to assault that city, and they decided that there should

be no delay in implementing this decision. They thought the results of this action would be in their favour. And since they would be in complete control after their successful conquest of Al-Madinah, they could run the administration as they pleased. As soon as they heard of the decision, all the chieftains became greedy for what was yet to fall into their laps! They started dreaming of the power of government and the might that would soon be theirs!

Abu Bakr Siddiq put into operation precautionary measures; he posted strict vigilance posses on all the roads leading into AlMadinah, to cope with all possible dangerous incursions. He had realised from the reactions of the members of the delegation that his very firm uncompromising reply would invoke some reaction, possibly an attack on Al-Madinah itself. The reading of the situation by Abu Bakr Siddig # turned out to be correct; as soon as the sun set, the apostates attacked. But on every road experienced, fearless and brave warriors had been deployed. The Muslims, defending their beloved city, defeated the attackers. Turning around, they fled. The Mujahideen, who were on camel back pursued them for a long distance. When the apostates realised that their lives were at stake, they started beating their drums violently before the camels. The camels reared up so badly that it became difficult to control them; they turned round and raced to Al-Madinah, stopping only when they reached their home ground. But, by the Grace of Allah Almighty, none of the Mujahideen suffered loss of life or property. The apostates, however, became encouraged and more confident since the Mujahideen, on their camels had turned round and gone back to AlMadinah. They planned another attack, but the Caliph Abu Bakr Siddiq @ was well aware of what he was up against.

At night he organised a troop of Mujahideen and decided he would lead them himself. On the right of this army would be Nu’man bin Magqran -#, and on the left would be his brother ‘Abdullah bin Magran 4. At the back of these troops, Suwaid bin Maqran # would be positioned as for purposes of safety. They set out in the latter part of the night and assaulted the enemy led by Abu Bakr Siddig in the early hours of dawn. The attack was so sudden and powerful that the enemy could not withstand it. As the sun rose up in the heavens they

were defeated and fled from the scene. Their leader, Habbal bin Khawaled Asdi was killed. This battle was fought at Dhi-Al-Qassah. Abu Bakr Siddig handed over the organization and administration of the area to the famous and respected chieftain of the Banu Mazeenah, Nu’man bin Maqran -#. He then returned to Al-Madinah.

Soon after this the Caliph, Abu Bakr Siddiq #%, led his army on an expedition to crush the apostasy in the tribes of Banu ‘Abas, Banu Bakr, and Banu Dheeban. He entered the area of strategic importance, Rabathah. Nu’man bin Maqran .% took part in this expedition as the right wing Commander of the Muslim army. The enemy again faced a clear and manifest defeat, and the Muslim forces returned triumphant to Al-Madinah.

“Tt is essential that you stay in Al-Madinah, and Sa’d bin Abu Wagqas +4 would be the most appropriate person to command the Muslim forces.”

“Faith and trust in Allah Almighty should be your strongest defence; before you enter the battlefield you should send a delegation of dignified and imposing persons to the Iranian Empire. Those people who saw them for the first time should be over-awed by their very presence; they should be fearless, eloquent, and learned.”

This delegation reached Madyan to see the Iranian Empire, Yazdajar, and asked for permission to meet with him. The Empire sat in his grand court on his throne in all his splendour and glory and asked that they be presented. Without paying any attention to all the pomp and grandeur, the members of the delegation walked straight up to Empire Yazdajar, seated on the royal throne. The Empire haughtily asked his interpreter to ask these people what they wanted from him. Then displaying the utmost pride and arrogance, he said:

“Tell these people that they are taking advantage of our decency. Do they not know how far is our reach and our power?”

The assembled court quivered in the dead silence that followed the expression of the Empire’s wrath in thunderous tones. The legs of the ministers and courtiers standing subserviently with folded arms trembled in abject fear. The Empire’s face had turned purple with rage and sparks seemed to fly from his eyes that had become like red-hot embers. Each member of the Muslim delegation stood calmly, watching the hysterical outburst of the Empire of Iran. When he had finished giving vent to his immature and childish tantrum, the head of the delegation, Nu’man bin Magqran -%, spoke to him after first reciting Verses in praise of Allah Almighty and the Prophet 3%, he said:

“O King of Iran listen carefully to what we have to say. Allah Almighty has been very Beneficent and Kind to us. He sent his Messenger to guide us; the Prophet 38 urged us to do good and avoid all kinds of evil. He also gave us the glad tidings that if we accept his invitation we will prosper both in this world and in the next. We accepted his invitation and within a few days our lives altered radically for the better. Our hand-to-mouth existence changed to a life of plenty; our shameful life was transformed into a life of respect and honour, and ail our petty jealousies, and rivalries were forgotten in feelings charged with fraternal love. Our Prophet 3 commanded us to teach people to be well wishes and to start this with those closest to us - our neighbours. This is why we are inviting you to enter the fold of Islam. The Islamic way of life lifts up its followers from the bottomless depths of kufr and association of other deities with the One True Allah, to the most exalted and radiant light of True Faith. If you accept this way of life we can leave with you the Book of Allah Almighty, the Noble Qur’an, and you can impose its faws on your people. We will not interfere in any matter of yours, we are not greedy for the material things of this world. If, however, you reject our invitation, then you have to be prepared to pay the Jazia, imposed by us on non-Muslims. If you are not prepared to do even this, then understand that the matter of who will rule over this land will have to be settled in the battlefield.”

The Iranian Empire was infuriated by the speech of Nu’man bin Magran and jumping up he said:

“There is no greater ignoramus than you on the surface of the earth; you do not even know in whose court you are chattering such drivel. We will entrust your matter to chiefs on our borders, and they will see to it. Do not advance even one inch into our territory. We are very capable of destroying you.”

Then calming down a little, he said:

“Yes, an idea has struck me. If some need or necessity has brought you to us, then we can give you enough grains to

provide your homes with food. Not only this, we will also present to your chieftains expensive and grand clothes. We will also appoint a ruler who will always treat you well. Tell me, what you would like to have?”

One of the members of the delegation bravely said:

“We do not care two hoots for your offerings of grains or expensive and grand clothes. You are drunk with the power of your monarchy, and you cannot see the throne wobbling insecurely under you. These courtly followers and sycophants and this worldly wealth and its luxuries will not be of any use in the long run. Our spiritual power will become a whip and a scourge for every Iranian. When we enter the battlefield we leap on our prey like lions and you are our favourite prey.”

He gave the Empire of Iran such a dressing down that he became even more enraged. Seething with fury, he said:

“Tf there had not been an international law regarding the security and safety of diplomats, I would have ordered you to be beheaded. Get out of my sight, I do not have anything for you. And listen! Go and_ tell your Commander-in-Chief that] am sending my General Rustum now towards you, he will bury you in the battlefield of Qadissiyah.”
content_id:
Delegasi itu kembali tanpa kata kepada para pemimpin mereka dan menyampaikan apa yang dikatakan Abu Bakr Siddiq 4. Mendengar jawaban ini, para pemimpin suku menjadi murka dan berpikir bahwa para pengikut Islam yang berada di Al-Madinah tidak akan mampu melawan mereka. Dengan demikian, ini adalah waktu yang tepat untuk menyerang kota itu, dan mereka memutuskan untuk tidak menunda pelaksanaan keputusan ini. Mereka mengira hasil dari tindakan ini akan menguntungkan mereka. Dan karena mereka akan menguasai sepenuhnya setelah penaklukan Al-Madinah yang berhasil, mereka bisa menjalankan pemerintahan sesuka hati mereka. Begitu mendengar keputusan itu, semua pemimpin suku menjadi tamak terhadap apa yang belum jatuh ke tangan mereka! Mereka mulai bermimpi tentang kekuasaan pemerintahan dan kekuatan yang akan segera menjadi milik mereka!

Abu Bakr Siddiq menerapkan langkah-langkah pencegahan; beliau menempatkan pos-pos pengawasan ketat di semua jalan yang menuju ke Al-Madinah, untuk menghadapi segala kemungkinan serangan berbahaya. Beliau telah menyadari dari reaksi para anggota delegasi bahwa jawabannya yang sangat tegas dan tanpa kompromi akan memicu reaksi tertentu, mungkin serangan terhadap Al-Madinah sendiri. Pembacaan situasi oleh Abu Bakr Siddiq ternyata benar; begitu matahari terbenam, kaum murtad menyerang. Namun di setiap jalan, para pejuang yang berpengalaman, tak kenal takut, dan pemberani telah ditempatkan. Kaum Muslimin, yang mempertahankan kota tercinta mereka, mengalahkan para penyerang. Mereka berbalik dan melarikan diri. Para mujahidin yang menunggang unta mengejar mereka dalam jarak yang jauh. Ketika kaum murtad menyadari bahwa nyawa mereka terancam, mereka mulai memukul genderang dengan keras di hadapan unta-unta itu. Unta-unta itu terkejut begitu hebat sehingga sulit untuk mengendalikannya; unta-unta itu berbalik dan berlari menuju Al-Madinah, baru berhenti ketika mencapai tempat asal mereka. Namun, dengan karunia Allah Yang Maha Kuasa, tidak seorang pun dari mujahidin yang mengalami kerugian jiwa atau harta. Kaum murtad, bagaimanapun, menjadi terdorong dan lebih percaya diri karena para mujahidin di atas unta mereka telah berbalik dan kembali ke Al-Madinah. Mereka merencanakan serangan lain, tetapi Khalifah Abu Bakr Siddiq sangat menyadari apa yang sedang dihadapinya.

Pada malam hari, beliau mengorganisir pasukan mujahidin dan memutuskan untuk memimpin mereka sendiri. Di sayap kanan pasukan ini adalah Nu'man bin Muqarran, dan di sayap kiri adalah saudaranya Abdullah bin Muqarran. Di belakang pasukan ini, Suwaid bin Muqarran ditempatkan untuk tujuan keamanan. Mereka berangkat pada bagian akhir malam dan menyerang musuh yang dipimpin oleh Abu Bakr Siddiq pada waktu fajar. Serangan itu begitu tiba-tiba dan dahsyat sehingga musuh tidak mampu menahannya. Ketika matahari terbit di langit, mereka

dikalahkan dan melarikan diri dari medan pertempuran. Pemimpin mereka, Habbal bin Khalid Al-Asdi, terbunuh. Pertempuran ini terjadi di Dzi Al-Qassah. Abu Bakr Siddiq menyerahkan organisasi dan administrasi wilayah tersebut kepada pemimpin suku Banu Muzainah yang terkenal dan dihormati, Nu'man bin Muqarran. Kemudian beliau kembali ke Al-Madinah.

Tidak lama setelah itu, Khalifah Abu Bakr Siddiq memimpin pasukannya dalam ekspedisi untuk menumpas kemurtadan di suku-suku Banu Abas, Banu Bakr, dan Banu Dzibyan. Beliau memasuki wilayah yang memiliki kepentingan strategis, yaitu Rabadzah. Nu'man bin Muqarran ikut serta dalam ekspedisi ini sebagai Komandan sayap kanan pasukan Muslim. Musuh kembali menghadapi kekalahan yang jelas dan nyata, dan pasukan Muslim kembali dengan penuh kemenangan ke Al-Madinah.

"Sangat penting bagi Anda untuk tetap di Al-Madinah, dan Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas adalah orang yang paling tepat untuk memimpin pasukan Muslim."

"Iman dan tawakkul kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa harus menjadi pertahanan terkuat kalian; sebelum kalian memasuki medan perang, kalian harus mengirim delegasi yang terdiri dari orang-orang yang berwibawa dan berpengaruh ke Kekaisaran Iran. Orang-orang yang melihat mereka untuk pertama kali harus merasa gentar dengan kehadiran mereka semata; mereka harus tak kenal takut, fasih berbicara, dan berilmu."

Delegasi ini sampai di Madyan untuk menemui Kaisar Iran, Yazdajird, dan meminta izin untuk bertemu dengannya. Kaisar duduk di istana megahnya di atas singgasana dengan segala kemegahan dan kemuliaannya dan meminta agar mereka dihadapkan. Tanpa menghiraukan semua kemewahan dan keagungan itu, para anggota delegasi berjalan langsung menuju Kaisar Yazdajird yang duduk di singgasana kerajaan. Kaisar dengan angkuh meminta penerjemahnya untuk menanyakan apa yang diinginkan orang-orang ini darinya. Kemudian dengan menunjukkan kesombongan dan keangkuhan yang luar biasa, ia berkata:

"Katakan kepada orang-orang ini bahwa mereka memanfaatkan kesopanan kami. Tidakkah mereka tahu seberapa jauh jangkauan dan kekuasaan kami?"

Para hadirin di istana gemetar dalam keheningan yang menyusul ungkapan murka Kaisar dengan nada yang menggelegar. Kaki para menteri dan para abdi istana yang berdiri tunduk dengan tangan terlipat bergetar dalam ketakutan yang amat sangat. Wajah Kaisar telah berubah menjadi merah padam karena kemarahan dan percikan api seakan terbang dari matanya yang telah menjadi seperti bara yang membara. Setiap anggota delegasi Muslim berdiri dengan tenang, menyaksikan luapan emosi yang histeris dari Kaisar Iran. Ketika ia selesai melampiaskan amarahnya yang tidak dewasa dan kekanak-kanakan, ketua delegasi, Nu'man bin Muqarran, berbicara kepadanya setelah terlebih dahulu membacakan ayat-ayat pujian kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa dan Nabi ﷺ, ia berkata:

"Wahai Raja Iran, dengarkanlah baik-baik apa yang hendak kami sampaikan. Allah Yang Maha Kuasa telah sangat Dermawan dan Baik kepada kami. Dia mengutus Rasul-Nya untuk membimbing kami; Nabi ﷺ mendesak kami untuk berbuat kebaikan dan menjauhi segala jenis kejahatan. Beliau juga memberi kami kabar gembira bahwa jika kami menerima ajakannya, kami akan beruntung baik di dunia maupun di akhirat. Kami menerima ajakannya dan dalam beberapa hari kehidupan kami berubah secara drastis menjadi lebih baik. Kehidupan kami yang serba kekurangan berubah menjadi kehidupan yang berkelimpahan; kehidupan kami yang penuh kehinaan berubah menjadi kehidupan yang terhormat dan bermartabat, dan semua kedengkian serta persaingan kecil kami dilupakan dalam perasaan yang dipenuhi kasih sayang persaudaraan. Nabi kami ﷺ memerintahkan kami untuk mengajarkan orang-orang agar menjadi penyeru kebaikan dan memulainya dari orang-orang yang terdekat dengan kami, yaitu tetangga-tetangga kami. Inilah mengapa kami mengajak Anda untuk memeluk Islam. Cara hidup Islam mengangkat para pengikutnya dari jurang kufur yang tak berdasar dan penyekutuan tuhan-tuhan lain dengan Allah Yang Maha Esa, menuju cahaya iman yang paling mulia dan bersinar terang. Jika Anda menerima cara hidup ini, kami dapat meninggalkan Kitab Allah Yang Maha Kuasa untuk Anda, yaitu Al-Quran yang Mulia, dan Anda dapat menerapkan hukum-hukumnya kepada rakyat Anda. Kami tidak akan mencampuri urusan Anda apa pun, kami tidak tamak terhadap hal-hal duniawi. Namun, jika Anda menolak ajakan kami, maka Anda harus bersiap untuk membayar jizyah yang kami kenakan kepada non-Muslim. Jika Anda tidak bersedia melakukan hal itu pun, maka ketahuilah bahwa persoalan siapa yang akan menguasai tanah ini harus diselesaikan di medan perang."

Kaisar Iran menjadi sangat murka mendengar pidato Nu'man bin Muqarran dan melompat berdiri seraya berkata:

"Tidak ada orang yang lebih bodoh daripada kalian di muka bumi ini; kalian bahkan tidak tahu di istana siapa kalian sedang mengoceh dengan omong kosong seperti ini. Kami akan menyerahkan urusan kalian kepada para pemimpin di perbatasan kami, dan mereka yang akan mengurusnya. Jangan maju selangkah pun ke wilayah kami. Kami sangat mampu menghancurkan kalian."

Kemudian sedikit tenang, ia berkata:

"Ya, sebuah gagasan terlintas di benakku. Jika ada kebutuhan atau keperluan yang membawa kalian kepada kami, maka kami dapat memberi kalian biji-bijian yang cukup untuk

menyediakan makanan bagi rumah-rumah kalian. Tidak hanya itu, kami juga akan menghadiahkan kepada para pemimpin kalian pakaian yang mahal dan megah. Kami juga akan mengangkat seorang penguasa yang akan selalu memperlakukan kalian dengan baik. Katakan kepadaku, apa yang kalian inginkan?"

Salah seorang anggota delegasi dengan berani berkata:

"Kami sama sekali tidak peduli dengan tawaran biji-bijian atau pakaian mahal dan megah dari Anda. Anda mabuk dengan kekuasaan kerajaan Anda, dan Anda tidak bisa melihat singgasana yang bergoyang tidak aman di bawah Anda. Para pengikut istana yang menjilat dan kekayaan duniawi beserta kemewahan ini tidak akan berguna dalam jangka panjang. Kekuatan spiritual kami akan menjadi cambuk dan momok bagi setiap orang Iran. Ketika kami memasuki medan perang, kami menerkam mangsa kami seperti singa, dan kalian adalah mangsa favorit kami."

Ia memberi Kaisar Iran teguran yang begitu keras sehingga ia menjadi semakin murka. Mendidih dengan kemarahan, ia berkata:

"Seandainya tidak ada hukum internasional mengenai keamanan dan keselamatan diplomat, aku sudah memerintahkan untuk memenggal kepala kalian. Pergi dari hadapanku, aku tidak punya apa pun untuk kalian. Dan dengarkan! Pergi dan katakan kepada Panglima Tertinggi kalian bahwa aku sekarang mengirim Jenderal Rustum ke arah kalian, dia akan mengubur kalian di medan perang Qadisiyyah."

On hearing this reply the chieftains were enraged and thought the followers of الإسلام present in AlMadinah would not be able to oppose them. وهكذا, this would be an opportune time to assault that city, and they decided that there should be no delay in implementing this decision. They thought the results of this action would be in their النعمة. And بما أن they would be in complete control بعد their successful الفتح of Al-المدينة المنورة, they could run the administration as they pleased. As soon as they heard of the decision, all the chieftains became greedy for what was yet to fall into their laps! They started dreaming of the power of government and the might that would soon be theirs! أبو بكر Siddiq put into operation precautionary measures; he posted strict vigilance posses on all the roads leading into AlMadinah, to cope with all possible dangerous incursions. He had realised from the reactions of the members of the delegation that his very firm uncompromising reply would invoke some reaction, possibly an attack on Al-المدينة المنورة itself. The reading of the situation by أبو بكر Siddig # turned out to be correct; as soon as the sun set, the apostates attacked. But on every road experienced, fearless and brave warriors had been deployed. The المسلمون, defending their beloved city, الهزيمةed the attackers. Turning around, they fled. The المجاهدين, who were on camel back pursued them for a long distance. عندما the apostates realised that their lives were at stake, they started beating their drums violently قبل the camels. The camels reared up لذا badly that it became difficult to control them; they turned round and raced to Al-المدينة المنورة, stopping only عندما they reached their home ground. But, by the الفضل of الله Almighty, none of the المجاهدين suffered loss of الحياة or property. The apostates, غير أن, became encouraged and more confident بما أن the المجاهدين, on their camels had turned round and gone back to AlMadinah. They planned another attack, but the Caliph أبو بكر Siddiq @ was well aware of what he was up against. At night he organised a troop of المجاهدين and decided he would lead them himself. On the الحق of this الجيش would be Nu’man bin Magqran -#, and on the left would be his الأخ ‘Abdullah bin Magran 4. At the back of these القوات, Suwaid bin Maqran # would be positioned أما purposes of safety. They set out in the latter part of the night and assaulted the العدو led by أبو بكر Siddig in the early hours of dawn. The attack was لذا sudden and powerful that the العدو could not withstand it. As the sun rose up in the heavens they were الهزيمةed and fled from the scene. Their leader, Habbal bin Khawaled Asdi was killed. This المعركة was قاتل at Dhi-Al-Qassah. أبو بكر Siddig handed over the organization and administration of the area to the famous and respected chieftain of the Banu Mazeenah, Nu’man bin Maqran -#. He then returned to Al-المدينة المنورة. Soon بعد this the Caliph, أبو بكر Siddiq #%, led his الجيش on an expedition to crush the apostasy in the tribes of Banu ‘Abas, Banu Bakr, and Banu Dheeban. He entered the area of strategic importance, Rabathah. Nu’man bin Maqran .% took part in this expedition as the الحق wing القائد of the مسلم الجيش. Those people who saw them for the أولاً time should be over-awed by their very presence; they should be fearless, eloquent, and learned.” This delegation reached Madyan to see the Iranian Empire, Yazdajar, and asked for permission to meet with him. The Empire haughtily asked his interpreter to ask these people what they wanted from him. Then displaying the utmost الكبر and الغرور, قال: “Tell these people that they are taking advantage of our decency. He sent his Messenger to guide us; النبي ﷺ 38 urged us to do الخير and avoid all kinds of الشر. He أيضاً gave us the glad tidings that إذا we accept his invitation we will prosper both in this الدنيا and in the next. Our النبي 3 commanded us to teach people to be well wishes and to start this with those closest to us - our الجيران. This is why we are inviting you to enter the fold of الإسلام. The Islamic الطريق of الحياة lifts up its followers from the bottomless depths of الكفر and association of other deities with the One True الله, to the most exalted and radiant light of True الإيمان. إذا you accept this الطريق of الحياة we can leave with you the الكتاب of الله Almighty, the Noble Qur’an, and you can impose its faws on your people. We will not interfere in any matter of yours, we are not greedy for the material things of this الدنيا. إذا, غير أن, you reject our invitation, then you have to be prepared to pay the Jazia, imposed by us on non-المسلمون. إذا you are not prepared to do even this, then understand that the matter of who will rule over this land will have to be settled in the المعركةfield.” The Iranian Empire was infuriated by the speech of Nu’man bin Magran and jumping up قال: “There is no greater ignoramus than you on the surface of the earth; you do not even know in whose court you are chattering such drivel. Not only this, we will أيضاً present to your chieftains expensive and grand clothes. We will أيضاً appoint a ruler who will always treat you well. Tell me, what you would like to have?” One of the members of the delegation ببسالة said: “We do not care two hoots for your offerings of grains or expensive and grand clothes. You are drunk with the power of your monarchy, and you cannot see the throne wobbling insecurely under you. These courtly followers and sycophants and this worldly الثروة and its luxuries will not be of any use in the long run. Seething with fury, قال: “Tf there had not been an international law فيما يخص the security and safety of diplomats, I would have ordered you to be beheaded. Get out of my sight, I do not have anything for you. And listen! Go and_ tell your القائد-in-Chief that] am sending my General Rustum now towards you, he will bury you in the المعركةfield of Qadissiyah.”
